mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates reduce discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 9.5 percent signal severely depressed consumer spending and weak corporate earnings growth, mechanically driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based aggregate demand recovery, mechanically increasing equity risk premiums and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month signals accelerating supply-side momentum that translates into short-term risk-on positioning and positive five-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.02) when inflation exceeds 3% or during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Policy rates near zero combined with annual inflation below two percent creates a persistently negative real yield environment, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in <40% of instances when both conditions hold, or real yields invert the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and consumption expenditures, mechanically boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive one-month equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding two percent confirms sustained economic expansion, triggering short-term institutional risk-on positioning and positive five-day equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative returns in >55% of triggered instances, or signal decays within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates improving capacity utilization without labor market overheating, mechanically supporting positive one-month equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline (Sharpe < 0.5) or reverses direction when inflation expectations rise above 2.5%.
